steps.title: Identify what carpa theater's stylistic influence means
steps.content: The historian describes this influence as “audience-mediated slapstick comedy.” This means two things: exaggerated physical humor (slapstick) where the audience's active reactions and participation were built into the performance itself.

steps.title: Find the choice showing slapstick and audience participation
steps.content: Choice D presents El Teatro performing with “exaggerated physical humor” to agricultural workers whose “reactions...promoted audience participation,” demonstrating exactly what the historian identifies as carpa theater's stylistic influence: slapstick comedy where audience reactions are integral. Choices A, B, and C discuss resources, messaging, or cultural aesthetic—not the interactive performance style carpa contributed. That matches choice D — D is correct.
